topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The late afternoon sun slanted through the window, painting stripes across the worn carpet. I blinked slowly, the world feeling thick and slow around the edges. A rhythmic tapping started at the property line—a persistent, insistent sound. I groaned softly, pulling the blanket tighter around my shoulders. *Just five more minutes,* I thought, my eyelids feeling heavy as lead weights. When I finally managed to lift my head, I saw him, standing by his overgrown hedge, holding some kind of measuring tape. He looked up, a bright, energetic smile on his face, clearly eager to discuss the perimeter.

[story 2]

My eyes kept drifting shut. The hum of the refrigerator was the loudest thing in the room, a low, monotonous drone that seemed to vibrate right through my bones. Outside, the sound of distant hammering began, punctuated by a neighbor’s cheerful voice. I shifted under the duvet, a deep, languid sigh escaping my lips. *This is too much effort,* my mind whispered, a sluggish current pulling me down. When I finally forced myself to sit up, the light filtering in seemed too intense, and the neighbor was already gesturing emphatically about posts and wire.

[story 3]

I felt a profound weight settling over my limbs, making every movement deliberate and sluggish. The sound of heavy machinery—or perhaps just someone dragging lumber—drifted over the fence line. I rubbed my eyes with the heels of my hands, trying to clear the hazy vision. *I really should just rest,* a quiet plea echoed in my head. When I finally opened my eyes wide enough to focus, the neighbor was mid-sentence, his voice high with enthusiasm as he explained the proposed boundary line. I managed a weak nod, my response barely audible.
